title: Auxiliary stop-horizon head: predict time-to-next-pit alongside next-lap pit
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop work reports a cascaded multi-task setup where a classification head is complemented by a regression head for laps-until-stop, and XGBoost’s AFT survival objective natively supports censored time-to-event labels.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop work reports a cascaded multi-task setup where a classification head is complemented by a regression head for laps-until-stop, and XGBoost’s AFT survival objective natively supports censored time-to-event labels. That is a closer formulation to the real strategy problem than pure binary next-lap classification.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-stint, derive `laps_to_next_pit` for training rows; use uncensored labels when a later pit exists and right-censored labels at race/stint end otherwise. Train either a separate AFT model or a two-stage model, then feed predicted remaining-laps/hazard summaries into the current boosters and blend OOF scores.
expected_effect: Should add a smoother notion of imminence, improving ranking for laps 1-5 before a stop rather than only the final lap.
risk: Label construction is easy to leak if you compute horizon features outside each training fold; all horizon labels and derived features must be built fold-locally.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
